title: Garlicky Tender Polish Garlic Bowl
description: A hearty one-pot Polish dish featuring tender meat, bold flavors, and a hint of Asian-chive-buchu, all within the realms of Whole30. Perfect for a weeknight dinner when you need a comforting meal without the fuss.
instructions: Heat coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the beef and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the browned beef from the pot and set it aside.
Add the onion and bell pepper to the pot and cook until they start to soften, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the caraway seeds, thyme, parsley, salt, and pepper to the pot. Stir to combine.
Add the browned beef back into the pot, along with the Asian-chive-buchu. Stir to combine.
Pour in enough beef broth to cover the meat, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the stew simmer, covered, for about 1 1/2 hours, or until the meat is tender.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
Serve over cauliflower rice or zucchini noodles for a low-carb option.
If desired, add some chopped fresh parsley or dill to the top for extra flavor and color.
